blurb:
If you have been looking for a solid Indian spot near Perimeter that is not trying too hard, this is it. The vibe is colorful and casual, and they serve everything family-style, which makes it easy to share and try a bunch of things. Good for a weeknight dinner when you want something flavorful without a lot of fuss.

The biryani is the reason most people come back. It is fragrant, filling, and pretty much what you want from a place that puts it in the name. The portions are generous and the price is reasonable for what you get.

They are open late on Friday and Saturday nights, which is nice if you are coming from an event nearby. Weekday hours start at 11:30am if you are thinking lunch.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is one of those places that earns its reputation. It sits right on the Chattahoochee, and the view alone makes the meal feel special. This is the spot for a real date night, an anniversary dinner, or any occasion where you want to impress someone without having to drive downtown.

The Southern seafood is the heart of the menu, and it is done well. The setting is intimate without feeling stuffy, so it works even if you are not normally a fine dining person. Sunday brunch is also worth knowing about if you want a more relaxed version of the experience.

It is on the pricier side, so plan accordingly. Reservations are a smart call, especially on weekends.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This is one of those spots that flys under the radar but has a loyal following for good reason. It's casual, the prices are low, and the food is the real deal. Good for a quick lunch or an easy weeknight dinner when you don't want to spend much.

The shawarma and falafel are what people keep coming back for. They also have solid veggie options, which is nice if you've got someone in your group who doesn't eat meat. Desserts are worth saving room for too.

It's open every day from 10am to 9pm, so it's pretty easy to work into your week.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
A 4.9 rating with over 5,000 reviews is hard to ignore, and Mister O1 backs it up. They do traditional Italian thin-crust pizza with ingredients that are either locally sourced or brought in from Italy. It is a good date night spot or a fun dinner out with friends when you want something a step above the usual pizza run.

The pizzas are the main event here, and the thin crust is the real deal. They also have salads and antipasti that are worth ordering to start. Nothing on the menu feels like an afterthought.

They are open until 11pm on Friday and Saturday if you need a later dinner option.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you need a place at 2am after a late night out, or just want a massive breakfast on a Sunday morning, Marietta Diner has you covered. It's open 24 hours, seven days a week, and the menu is enormous. The neon sign out front pretty much tells you everything you need to know about the vibe inside.

People come here for the big breakfasts and the Greek specialties mixed in with all your classic diner fare. The portions are generous and the prices are fair for what you get. It's the kind of place where you can bring the whole family, a group of friends after a game, or just yourself when you need a plate of eggs and nowhere else is open.

Parking is easy and there's almost always a seat available no matter what time you show up.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours

blurb:
Douceur De France is the kind of place you tell people about and they immediately ask why they haven't heard of it sooner. It's a French bakery and brunch spot on South Marietta Pkwy with a 4.9 rating from over 16,000 reviews, which should tell you everything. Perfect for a weekend morning or a slow weekday breakfast before work.

The baguettes, quiches, and petits fours are the things people rave about. It's bright inside and the kind of spot where you actually want to sit down and slow down for a bit. Definitely go earlier in the day if you want the best selection.

They close at 6pm on weekdays and at 3:30pm on Sundays, so plan accordingly.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA

blurb:
If you haven't been to Rio yet, put it on your list. It's an all-you-can-eat Brazilian barbecue spot off Powers Ferry, and it's a genuinely fun night out. Good for birthdays, date night, or just when you want to eat way too much with good company.

The barbecue is the main draw, but the cheese bread alone is worth the trip. They also do feijoada stew and a full breakfast setup depending on when you go. The price is reasonable for how much food you get.

They're open late on Fridays and Saturdays until 11pm, which makes it a solid option if you're eating on the later side.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Yalda has become one of those places people are genuinely excited to take friends who have never tried Persian food. The atmosphere is warm and the service makes you feel taken care of all night. It works great for a girls night out, a date, or any dinner where you want the full experience from start to finish.

Start with the hummus and the wings, then move on to the chicken shish kabobs served with rice with lentils and raisins. The cocktails are good across the board, and finish with the tiramisu if you want dessert. It is one of those meals where you leave already thinking about coming back.

Note that they close between lunch and dinner on weekdays, so there is a gap from around 2:45pm to 4pm. Plan around that if your schedule is tight.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places the neighborhood keeps to itself but probably should not. The vibe is loud and fun, the service is attentive, and the food actually lives up to what people say about it. Great for a group dinner or a casual night out when you want good food and a couple of good drinks.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth ordering, especially if you are sharing with the table. They are refreshing and well-balanced without being too sweet.

They are open until midnight every night except Sunday, so it is also a solid late-night option if you need one.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Marietta Square Market is a food hall right on the Square with around twenty different vendors under one roof. It's perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat, or when you just want to explore a little. The vibe is lively and social without being too loud.

Everyone splits up, grabs what they want from different stalls, and meets back at a table. You'll find all kinds of cuisines in one place, which makes it great for families or mixed groups. It's also a solid spot for a casual lunch if you're already spending time around the Square.

Hours are 11am to 9pm most days and until 10pm on Fridays and Saturdays.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
